# backend/ws_broadcast/router.py
# auto-generated explainer · 2026-06-12T10:01:56Z

## Purpose
/ws/activity · Iter 34 · C9 simplified · server-side broadcast WebSocket.

Operator clients connect to /ws/activity · server pushes JSON events.
Per §57.7: stays connected · sends keepalive · doesn't pretend to receive.

## Imports
- from `__future__` import annotations
- from `_` import asyncio
- from `_` import json
- from `_` import logging
- from `datetime` import datetime, timezone
- from `fastapi` import APIRouter, WebSocket, WebSocketDisconnect

## Inputs
(no CLI / env inputs — library module or implicit)

## Process
### Functions
- `async broadcast(message)`: Send to all connected clients · return count delivered.
- `async activity_channel(ws)`: Long-lived push channel for activity events.
- `list_clients()`: Operational view · how many WS clients connected.

## Outputs
- Return values from functions above

## Notable inline comments
- Track connected clients
- Keepalive loop · keeps the connection healthy through proxies
